div1.entryFree:
Painfully, 1) with distress of the body or mind: within which rift thou didst p. remain a dozen years, Tp. I, 2, 278. thou hast p. discovered, Tim. V, 2, 1 (so as to distress us by the result of thy discovery).
2) laboriously: p. to pore upon a book, LLL I, 1, 74. who p. have brought a countercheck, John II, 223.
